short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · next earnings cycle)
bullets: Run-rate $1.41B in FY2026 Q1, -30.5% YoY — contracting trajectory entering the next print.
Next-quarter consensus: $1.95B (+38.3% vs current Q) — set by 30 broker contributors.
EPS consensus next qtr: $2.10.
Next earnings: 2026-07-30 — primary catalyst for the short-term window.
short_term.takeaway: Going into the next earnings print, the setup is mixed — execution risk on the next guide is elevated.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · multi-year setup)
bullets: TTM revenue $7B — in cyclical drawdown.
Sell-side: FY1 $8B → FY2 $8B (+11.8% multi-year top-line implied).
Forward EBIT margin: 35.0% — structural premium business over the long-cycle view.
6-quarter run-rate CAGR: -2.0% — the secular trajectory the market is pricing.
Sector thesis (Financials): Rate-cycle position + capital-return runway define the 1-3 year setup.
long_term.takeaway: Long-cycle thesis is intact if the next 2–4 quarters confirm a stabilization in trajectory.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · trading window)
bullets: Price $184.99 vs SMA50 $191.08 (-3.2%) — short-term trend is bearish (price has lost the SMA50 line).
RSI(14): 40.9 — neutral / no edge.
MACD: 0.06 vs signal 2.14 — bearish crossover.
30-day return: -7.4% — directional bias entering the next 4 weeks.
Bollinger band position: 17% of band width (band width 17.7% — normal).
Near-term S/R: resistance ~$190.54, support ~$179.44. 16% of 52w range (low $141.09 → high $419.78).
short_term.takeaway: Short-term trader bias: short (trend + momentum aligned bearish).

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · positioning window)
bullets: Price $184.99 vs SMA200 $251.12 (-26.3%) — long-term trend is in **downtrend**.
Moving average regime: Death cross active (SMA50 < SMA200) — institutional trend filter bearish.
1-year return: -28.6% — in distribution.
Drawdown from 52w high: -55.9% — bear-market drawdown (contrarian setup).
Cycle S/R: long-term resistance $419.78 (52w high), structural support $141.09 (52w low).
90-day return: +2.2% — positive intermediate-term momentum.
long_term.takeaway: Long-term positioning: distribute on strength — trend has rolled over.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–45 DTE · front-month tactical)
bullets: Front-month ATM IV: 81.5% — extreme (binary-event hedging implied).
Put/Call OI: 0.53 — call-heavy positioning (consensus bullish).
30-day implied move: ±15.6% ($156.13 – $213.85) — the 1-σ range the options market is pricing.
ATM skew: +2.08pp — put skew (downside hedges bid).
Front-month OI cluster: $232.5 — likely magnet / pin-risk for expiry.
short_term.strategy: Iron condor 30 DTE: short $176 put / short $194 call (wings 5% wide). Range-bound + high IV → premium-selling with capped risk both sides.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ DTE · LEAPS / structural)
bullets: Back-month avg IV (90+ DTE): 71.5% across 8 expiries.
Available long-dated expiries: 8 dates spanning 91–609 DTE.
90-day implied move: ±35.4% ($119.45 – $250.53).
210-day implied move: ±54.4% ($84.35 – $285.63) — the long-dated 1-σ range.
Sell-side PT vs options-implied range: consensus $230 (+24.3% upside). Compare to the 90+ DTE implied range above.
Term structure in backwardation (-9.9pp front→back) — near-term event premium; long-dated vol cheaper.
long_term.strategy: Sell 6-month strangle: short $166 put / short $203 call. Range-bound + high IV → premium-selling on both sides; profits if spot stays within ±10%; manage with rolls or assignment.

fundamentals: Revenue -30.5% YoY (contracting for Financials; sector strong-bar 6%)

⚠ Value-trap pattern: cheap PE + below 200-DMA + sub-3% growth + negative 1Y return
→ V capped at 45 (cheap for a reason, not opportunity)
